response:
k is a key concept when solving this array problem because the index k determines if we square the difference or take the absolute value. for every third element where the index k is a multiple of three we must compute the sum of squared differences instead of the standard absolute sum. this twist changes the calculation logic significantly for specific positions in the sorted array.

***

the solution requires iterating through the array while tracking prefix sums to handle the large constraints efficiently without timing out. when the current index k satisfies the condition of being divisible by three we apply the square formula to all pairwise differences. otherwise we use the linear absolute difference formula to build the final result array quickly.
